message:
feat(storage): implement git-backed archive storage layer

Replace stub storage types with a full git-backed archive implementation
providing per-project repositories with advisory locks and commit queuing.

Core functionality:
- ensure_archive_root / ensure_archive: initialize archive directory
  structure with .gitattributes and git repo per project
- write_agent_profile / write_agent_profile_with_config: persist agent
  identity JSON under agents/<Name>/profile.json with auto-commit
- write_message_bundle: full message write pipeline — canonical copy under
  messages/YYYY/MM/, sender outbox, per-recipient inbox copies, all
  committed atomically
- write_file_reservation_records / write_file_reservation_record: persist
  reservation artifacts as both sha1(pattern).json and id-<id>.json under
  file_reservations/, with auto-commit
- emit_notification_signal / clear_notification_signal / list_pending_signals:
  lightweight file-based notification system for cross-agent coordination
- get_recent_commits: retrieve commit history with optional author/path
  filters and configurable limits
- collect_lock_status: diagnostic reporting of advisory lock states
- resolve_archive_relative_path: safe path resolution preventing traversal
  outside the archive root

Infrastructure:
- StorageError enum with variants for IO, Git, JSON, lock contention,
  invalid path, and uninitialized archive
- Advisory file locking via .archive.lock with stale-lock detection
- Commit queue with batching via CommitQueue (Mutex-protected) to reduce
  git lock contention from concurrent agents
- SHA1-based path hashing for reservation artifact deduplication
- now_iso() helper for consistent ISO-8601 timestamps

Testing:
- Add tempfile dev-dependency for isolated test repos
- Unit tests for archive initialization, agent profile writes, message
  bundle writes, file reservation writes, notification signals, commit
  queue batching, path resolution safety, and lock status collection

Also sync beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-1bm.9","title":"Health + Well-Known Endpoints Parity Tests","description":"Objective:
Verify health and well-known endpoints match legacy behavior and remain stable. Ensure status codes and JSON payloads are identical.

Endpoints:
- GET /health/liveness -> {"status":"alive"}
- GET /health/readiness -> {"status":"ready"} or 503 {"detail":"..."}
- GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server -> {"mcp_oauth": false}
- GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server/mcp -> {"mcp_oauth": false}

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
- Unit tests for readiness error formatting
- Integration tests for each endpoint and method mismatch (405)
- E2E script: hit each endpoint, log status/body, fail on mismatch

Acceptance Criteria:
- JSON payloads and status codes identical to legacy
- E2E logs show pass/fail for each endpoint","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:18.747115290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:18.747115290Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.9","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:18.747115290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei","title":"MCP Agent Mail Rust: 100% feature parity + conformance + benchmarks","description":"Goal: complete the Rust port of legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code with 100% behavior/feature coverage, proven by fixture-based conformance tests + benchmarks (same bar as rich_rust/beads_rust).

Non-negotiables:
- Prefer local crates over upstream ecosystem where applicable:
  - MCP: /dp/fastmcp_rust
  - SQLite: /dp/sqlmodel_rust
  - IO/concurrency: /dp/asupersync (avoid tokio unless unavoidable)
  - Console UI: /dp/frankentui
  - Beads: /dp/beads_rust
  - Agent detection: /dp/coding_agent_session_search
- No destructive commands or file deletions without explicit permission.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo fmt --check passes
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- cargo test passes
- Conformance harness covers all MCP tools + resources (including error cases and nondeterministic-field normalization)
- Benchmarks cover DB/tool hot paths and, once implemented, archive write throughput
","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:05:51.223489285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:05:51.223489285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-2ei.1","title":"Conformance Harness: cover all tools/resources + artifact parity","description":"Expand conformance tests so they prove real feature parity, not just API-shape parity.

Scope:
- Ensure python_reference.json includes cases for every MCP tool registered by Rust server (currently missing: force_release_file_reservation, install_precommit_guard, uninstall_precommit_guard).
- Add negative/error cases per tool where legacy behavior is defined (invalid params, CONTACT_BLOCKED flows, etc).
- Add resource query-param variants where legacy supports them.
- Add artifact-level assertions once storage/archive layer exists (message markdown files, inbox/outbox copies, file_reservation JSON artifacts, attachment manifests).

Acceptance:
- `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-conformance` fails on any behavioral drift.
- Fixtures are deterministic: normalize/ignore only genuinely nondeterministic fields (timestamps, hashes), and document why.

Key files:
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/python_reference/generate_fixtures.py
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/fixtures/python_reference.json
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance.rs

Missing tools (as of current python_reference.json):
- force_release_file_reservation
- install_precommit_guard
- uninstall_precommit_guard

Work:
- Update legacy python fixture generator to exercise these tools (use temp git repo for guard install/uninstall).
- Update Rust conformance runner to execute these calls.
- Decide normalization strategy for nondeterministic fields (timestamps, paths).

Dependencies:
- Requires real implementations in Rust (guard + force_release) so comparisons are meaningful.

This issue is a meta-epic; individual tools get their own child tasks with concrete fixture scenarios, normalization rules, and Rust runner integration.

Missing tools to cover:
- install_precommit_guard
- uninstall_precommit_guard
- force_release_file_reservation

Non-negotiables:
- Add BOTH positive and negative/error-path cases.
- Record expected shapes from legacy Python and keep fixtures deterministic (normalize only true nondeterminism like timestamps/paths).
- Include detailed logs in the fixture generator so failures are diagnosable.

Child tasks under this epic should each include:
- Legacy behavior matrix (inputs -> expected outputs)
- Fixture generator changes
- Rust conformance runner changes
- Any required normalization rules documented inline","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:10:18.916319777Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:51:37.895534917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.1.2","title":"Fixtures: add negative/error cases (invalid params, contact enforcement, policy coercions)","description":"Add high-signal error cases to conformance so we lock in edge behavior.

Candidates:
- ensure_project: relative path -> error
- register_agent: invalid name under strict enforcement -> error or coercion depending on config
- send_message: CONTACT_BLOCKED / CONTACT_REQUIRED flows
- file_reservation_paths: conflicts returned shape; TTL min enforcement
- renew_file_reservations: invalid ids/paths
- search_messages: FTS syntax vs LIKE fallback edge cases
- resources: missing required query params -> error

Approach:
- Add cases to python fixture generator where legacy behavior is clear.
- Normalize only nondeterministic fields.

Acceptance:
- Tests fail on any drift.

 {"id":"br-2ei.1.3","title":"Conformance: assert git archive artifacts (messages/inbox/outbox/reservations/attachments) match legacy","description":"Extend conformance beyond JSON tool outputs to verify archive side effects.

Artifacts to assert after running a fixture script:
- messages/YYYY/MM/*.md canonical exists; parses frontmatter JSON; body matches expected.
- agents/<Agent>/inbox + outbox copies exist and match canonical.
- file_reservations/*.json artifacts exist for created reservations.
- attachments manifests + WebP/originals as expected.

Strategy:
- Fixture generator should record expected relative paths and key frontmatter fields, not absolute machine paths.
- Use hashing for large binaries; compare sha256.
- Allow nondeterministic timestamps but assert ordering and presence.

Dependencies:
- Requires storage layer implementation.

 {"id":"br-2ei.2","title":"Storage: Git archive write/read pipeline + attachments","description":"Implement the git-backed mailbox archive described in EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md.

This is the core missing subsystem: legacy Python writes messages + file reservations + attachments into a per-project git repo and uses it as an auditable artifact store.

Scope (must match legacy behavior):
- Archive root creation + .gitattributes
- Per-project advisory locks (.archive.lock + owner metadata)
- Commit batching queue (max 10, max wait 50ms)
- Message write pipeline:
  - canonical messages/YYYY/MM/*.md
  - agents/<Agent>/inbox + outbox copies
  - thread digest files (messages/threads/<thread_id>.md) if present in legacy
  - git commit message format
- File reservation artifacts (file_reservations/<sha1(pattern)>.json + id-<id>.json)
- Attachment pipeline:
  - compute content hashes
  - convert images to WebP when enabled
  - inline small attachments (< 64KiB) into markdown
  - keep originals when configured
  - write manifests + audit logs
- Read helpers for resources that include commit metadata.

Constraints:
- Prefer git2 library (already in workspace deps); avoid shelling out when possible.
- Prefer asupersync for IO/concurrency.

Acceptance:
- New conformance layer can assert archive artifacts exactly match legacy (file layout + frontmatter JSON + markdown body).
- Benchmarks can measure write throughput and commit batching.

-{"id":"br-2ei.2.1","title":"Archive: initialize archive root + per-project git repos + .gitattributes","description":"Implement archive initialization in mcp-agent-mail-storage.

Requirements:
- Determine archive root from Config.storage_root (default ~/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo).
- Ensure root exists.
- For each project slug, ensure a git repo exists at <root>/<slug>/.
- Ensure expected top-level dirs exist: agents/, messages/, attachments/, file_reservations/.
- Write .gitattributes (at least: enforce LF for *.md and binary for images).
- Ensure git author identity set from config (name/email) for commits.

Implementation:
- Use git2 to init and commit initial scaffolding if repo newly created.

Acceptance:
- Idempotent: calling multiple times does not change repo.
- Unit test with temp dir.

 {"id":"br-2ei.2.2","title":"Archive: per-project advisory locks + commit batching queue","description":"Implement concurrency control for archive writes.

Spec:
- Per-project advisory lock file: <project>/.archive.lock (+ optional owner metadata JSON).
- Commit queue batching: max 10 commits or max wait 50ms.
- Stale lock cleanup on startup.

Implementation notes:
- Use filesystem lock (cross-platform) + busy-timeout retry. Prefer asupersync primitives if available.
- Keep API simple for callers: `with_project_lock(project_slug, || ...)`.
- Batching should preserve ordering for operations within a single process.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests simulate concurrent lock acquisition.
- Bench: lock acquisition overhead is low.
","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:09:10.275499179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:09:14.942264511Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.2.3","title":"Archive: message write pipeline (canonical + inbox/outbox + commit) + frontmatter format","description":"Implement message archival writes that mirror legacy Python.

Spec (EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md):
- Canonical: messages/YYYY/MM/<timestamp>__<subject_slug>__<id>.md
- Outbox: agents/<sender>/outbox/YYYY/MM/... (same rel path)
- Inboxes: agents/<recipient>/inbox/YYYY/MM/...
- Frontmatter: JSON preceded by ---json header line, then Markdown body.
- Git commit summary: "message <id> from <sender> to <recipients>".

Inputs:
- Message record + recipients + markdown body + attachments metadata.

Outputs:
- Files written atomically.
- Git commit performed (or batched) and commit info stored/returned where needed.

Acceptance:
- Deterministic path + slug generation.
- Conformance can assert files exist and parse.

 {"id":"br-2ei.2.4","title":"Archive: file reservation artifacts (sha1(pattern).json + id-<id>.json)","description":"Implement archive writes for file reservations.

Spec:
- file_reservations/<sha1(path_pattern)>.json
- file_reservations/id-<id>.json

Requirements:
- JSON includes {id, agent, path_pattern, exclusive, reason, created_ts, expires_ts, released_ts} at minimum.
- Writes are atomic.
- Archive writes happen on reservation create/update/release and are committed (or batched).

Acceptance:
- Resource `resource://file_reservations/{slug}` can be backed by archive artifacts (optional) and/or DB.
- Conformance can assert artifact existence.

@@ -55,7 +55,7 @@
 {"id":"br-2ei.3.1","title":"Guard: resolve repo + hooks dir (core.hooksPath, worktrees) via git2","description":"Implement robust hooks directory resolution.

Requirements:
- Input: repo working tree path (code_repo_path).
- Discover git repo (supports worktrees).
- Determine hooks dir:
  - If core.hooksPath is set: resolve relative to repo root per git semantics.
  - Else: use default hooks dir for the repository common dir.
- Return resolved hooks_dir as a real filesystem path.

Must handle:
- .git is a file containing gitdir: ...
- worktrees where hooks live in common dir
- bare repo (if encountered) -> treat as invalid for hook install

Deliverable:
- `mcp_agent_mail_guard::resolve_hooks_dir(repo: &Path) -> GuardResult<PathBuf>` (or equivalent internal helper) with unit tests.
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:07:47.277296976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:18:05.735479435Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:18:05.735461692Z","close_reason":"Implemented resolve_hooks_dir via git2 + commondir parsing; added unit tests incl. worktree case.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.3.2","title":"Guard: install/uninstall chain-runner hooks + agent-mail plugin scripts","description":"Implement install/uninstall mechanics (pre-commit and optionally pre-push).

Install behavior (match legacy):
- Ensure hooks.d/<hook>/ directory exists.
- Write/overwrite top-level hook script (chain-runner) that executes hooks.d/<hook>/* in lexical order.
- Preserve existing non-chain hook as <hook>.orig.
- Install our plugin under hooks.d/<hook>/50-agent-mail (or equivalent) that executes the actual guard check.
- Create Windows shims (.cmd/.ps1) if needed to invoke the chain-runner.

Uninstall behavior:
- Remove our plugins (hooks.d/<hook>/50-agent-mail.*).
- Only remove chain-runner if it is ours AND there are no other plugins remaining AND no .orig restoration is needed.
- If .orig exists and chain-runner is removed, restore it.

Deliverables:
- `install_guard(project_key, repo)` writes files and chmods +x on unix.
- `uninstall_guard(repo)` removes/restores safely.
- Unit tests use temp git repos.
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:08:05.382529734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:25:45.469594914Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:25:45.469578243Z","close_reason":"Implemented install_guard/uninstall_guard: resolves hooks dir, installs python chain-runner + Windows shims, installs plugin stub, preserves/restores existing hook via .orig; added unit test for preserve/restore.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.3.3","title":"Guard: implement conflict detection (reservations + path matching + rename handling)","description":"Implement the actual guard decision logic.

Inputs:
- repo path
- list of paths to check (from staged diff for pre-commit, from commit range for pre-push)
- AGENT_NAME env var identifies current agent
- mode: block vs warn (AGENT_MAIL_GUARD_MODE)
- bypass env: AGENT_MAIL_BYPASS=1

Logic:
- If guard disabled (WORKTREES_ENABLED or GIT_IDENTITY_ENABLED false): allow.
- Load active file reservations for the project identity associated with repo (project_identity_mode logic).
- If any exclusive reservation matches any path (symmetric matching) and reservation holder != AGENT_NAME and unexpired: conflict.
- For renames: treat both old and new paths as touched.

Deliverables:
- Pure function that returns conflicts (pattern, holder, expires_ts) for a given path list.
- Helper to obtain staged paths from git: use `git diff --cached --name-only -z --diff-filter=ACMRDTU` and rename pairs from `--name-status -M -z`.
- Helper to obtain pre-push paths from stdin refs (`git rev-list`) with fallback to `git diff --name-status -M -z <remote>..<local>`.

Tests:
- Synthetic reservations + path lists.
- Temp repo with staged rename.

+{"id":"br-2ei.3.4","title":"Guard Tools: install_precommit_guard/uninstall_precommit_guard wiring","description":"Wire MCP tools to the Rust guard crate (fixture generation + conformance assertions live under br-2ei.1.*; keep this issue implementation-only).

Tools:
- install_precommit_guard(project_key, code_repo_path)
- uninstall_precommit_guard(code_repo_path)

Requirements:
- Call into mcp-agent-mail-guard for repo discovery + hooks dir resolution.
- Idempotent + safe:
  - Install preserves existing hooks and writes chain-runner + agent-mail plugin(s).
  - Uninstall removes only agent-mail components; restores preserved hooks when applicable.
- Error parity:
  - Invalid repo paths, non-repo dirs, permission errors map to the same error semantics as legacy.
- Output parity:
  - JSON-RPC result shape must match legacy fixtures exactly.

Tests:
- Unit tests: parameter validation + error mapping.
- Integration test: temp git repo, run tool handler install then uninstall, assert expected hook files created/removed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:08:32.548948635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:49:00.627558674Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.3.4","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.3.4","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.3.5","title":"Guard: add unit + integration tests (git repos, worktrees, hooksPath, conflicts)","description":"Add focused tests to ensure guard behavior remains correct.

Tests should cover:
- hooks dir resolution: default vs core.hooksPath vs worktree common dir.
- install/uninstall idempotency and preservation of existing hooks.
- path matching: pathspec (if enabled) vs fnmatch fallback.
- conflict detection: exclusive reservations by other agent blocks; shared reservations do not.
- rename pairs considered touched.

Success:
- `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-guard` provides strong coverage.

 {"id":"br-2ei.4","title":"Share/Export: static bundle pipeline (SQLite snapshot + scrub + attachments + signing/encryption)","description":"Port legacy share/export pipeline to Rust (mcp-agent-mail-share + CLI).

Spec source:
- EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md section "Share / Export Pipeline (Static Bundle)"

Scope:
- Snapshot SQLite safely (WAL checkpoint + sqlite backup) into export DB.
- Apply project scoping (delete rows from other projects).
- Scrub presets: standard/strict/archive.
- Build FTS + materialized views; add indexes.
- Finalize DB for export (journal_mode=DELETE, page_size=1024, VACUUM, ANALYZE).
- Bundle attachments:
  - Inline <= 64KiB
  - Detach >= 25MiB
  - Copy others into attachments/<sha256[:2]>/<sha256>.<ext>
  - Rewrite messages.attachments JSON to bundle paths / data URIs
- Chunk DB if >= 20MiB.
- Bundle scaffolding: manifest.json, README, deploy hints, _headers.
- Optional: sign manifest (Ed25519), encrypt bundle (age), package zip.

Constraints:
- Prefer sqlmodel_rust for SQLite operations.
- Prefer asupersync for IO.

Acceptance:
- CLI commands work end-to-end on sample project.
- Bundle is deterministic for same inputs (stable ordering + hashing).
- Benchmarks for export path (time + output size).

 {"id":"br-2ei.4.1","title":"Share: SQLite snapshot + project scoping (WAL checkpoint + backup)","description":"Implement the first half of share/export.

Work:
- Open live DB with sqlmodel.
- WAL checkpoint.
- Use sqlite backup API to create an export DB file.
- Apply project scope: delete rows for non-selected projects.

Acceptance:
- Export DB is consistent + passes PRAGMA integrity_check.
- Scoped DB contains only requested projects.
